title: salvia pinguifolia
intro: Salvia pinguifolia, also known as blue pineleaf sage, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid. The health benefits of Salvia pinguifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in culinary applications due to its pine-like flavor.
